title: RapidMeta Dermatology | Bimekizumab (IL-17A/F Dual) in Plaque Psoriasis v1.0

pico.pop: Adults with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis (PASI >=12, BSA >=10%, IGA >=3)
pico.int: Bimekizumab 320 mg SC every 4 weeks (humanized IgG1 mAb, dual IL-17A and IL-17F neutralization)
pico.comp: Placebo (BE-VIVID, BE-READY) or adalimumab 80/40 mg SC (BE-SURE active-controlled head-to-head)
pico.subgroup: Prior biologic exposure, baseline PASI severity, BMI, body weight

real_data_entries.name: BE-VIVID
real_data_entries.group: Moderate-severe plaque psoriasis, bimekizumab 320 mg Q4W vs placebo arm of 3-arm RCT (3:2:1 bimi:ustek:placebo); ustekinumab arm reported separately

allOutcomes.title: Proportion achieving IGA 0/1 (clear/almost clear) at week 16

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Reich K et al. Lancet 2021;397:487-498 (BE-VIVID).

real_data_entries.name: BE-READY
real_data_entries.group: Moderate-severe plaque psoriasis, bimekizumab 320 mg Q4W vs placebo (4:1 randomization)
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Gordon KB et al. Lancet 2021;397:475-486 (BE-READY).

real_data_entries.name: BE-SURE
real_data_entries.group: Moderate-severe plaque psoriasis, bimekizumab 320 mg (Q4W and Q4W->Q8W combined) vs adalimumab 80/40 mg (active-controlled head-to-head)

allOutcomes.title: Proportion achieving PASI 90 at week 16 (primary, vs adalimumab)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Reich K et al. NEJM 2021;385:142-152 (BE-SURE bimekizumab vs adalimumab).
